mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures the momentum of consumer demand; stronger retail sales translate into higher expected corporate revenues and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A negative rank correlation between month-over-month RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns persists for more than 12 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point or greater increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a three-month span signals rapid labor market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and compresses corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE has risen by more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and positive consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) produces deeply negative real interest rates, which stimulates equity investment by reducing the real cost of capital and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through a joint effect exceeding either variable in isolation.
failure_condition: Portfolios sorted on the joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction signal fail to show positive monotonic returns across quintiles, or the top quintile does not outperform the bottom quintile (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In post-recession recovery regimes, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals reviving manufacturing momentum and rising capacity utilization, which lifts earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During months classified as post-recession recovery, month-over-month INDPRO growth fails to exhibit a positive rank correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts (HOUST) crossing above 750 thousand units indicates a recovering residential construction sector that stimulates demand for building materials, labor, and consumer durables, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 750 thousand units on a monthly basis
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of months when HOUST exceeds 750 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding employment and wage income, which supports aggregate demand and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show a negative rank correlation with 60-day forward returns for 18 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Joint declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) generate a reinforcing recession signal that is stronger than either indicator alone, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations reset lower.
failure_condition: During periods when INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During zero lower bound regimes where the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is held below 0.25%, the absence of near-term monetary tightening risk reduces the equity discount rate and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, the correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS and 60-day forward returns is not negative and significant (p < 0.05), or returns are negative in more than 40% of months.
